title: Run Agent Stack Episode 1 through distribution engine
description: Take Episode 1 (MCP) through complete distribution workflow. Use video-distributor skill to generate all platform variants (YouTube/Twitter/LinkedIn/Instagram/TikTok/Substack). Use lead-capture-optimizer for CTAs. Review outputs, upload to all platforms, schedule posts. Monitor first 48 hours of engagement.

title: Create '10 Agent Patterns' lead magnet PDF
description: Design and write 2-3 page PDF with 10 high-value agent patterns (quick wins). Set up email capture form using ConvertKit or Mailchimp. Create download landing page. Include examples from JARVIS (investment monitoring, content production, research automation).

title: Build lead-capture-optimizer skill
description: Generates platform-specific CTAs for each video clip. Creates links to: lead magnet download, course landing page, consulting booking page, Substack subscription. Optimizes CTA format for each platform's norms (pinned comments, thread endings, article CTAs, etc.).

title: Build video-distributor skill
description: Takes 1 long-form video and outputs platform-optimized clips + captions + thumbnails for YouTube/Twitter/LinkedIn/Instagram/TikTok/Substack. Each platform gets content optimized for its pacing, hooks, and format. Outputs 15-20 clips total per input video.

title: Document distribution workflow for Episodes 2-4
description: Create repeatable checklist/runbook for distribution process. Identify what can be fully automated vs needs human review. Document lessons learned from Episode 1. Goal: reduce time per episode to under 2 hours for Episodes 2-4.

title: Set up analytics dashboard for video distribution
description: Track key metrics: video views per platform (YouTube/Twitter/LinkedIn/etc.), lead magnet downloads, discovery call bookings, email list growth. Use Google Sheets + simple formulas or build basic dashboard. Update weekly.

title: Fix June 9 missing AI News Digest

Hardened repo and installed news wrapper to make scheduled email delivery explicit, fail loudly on mail/config issues, and keep vector reindex from blocking the digest job.

title: Rewrite Sunday YT scraper: RSS-based multi-video catch + correct titles + DB cleanup
description: Two bugs found in skills/wiki-ingest/check-claude-code-videos.py: (1) only most recent video per channel ingested per Sunday run — multi-publish weeks lose videos; (2) title regex matches a YouTube UI element ('Combinaciones de teclas') instead of real video titles. Fix: switch get_latest_video() to YouTube RSS feed (https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=<ID>), iterate entries, ingest all newer than last_video_id, ingest oldest-first so state ends on newest. RSS provides clean titles, fixes bug 2 automatically. Also clean up 9 DB rows (ids 270-273 from 2026-05-10 and 289-293 from 2026-05-15) plus their .md files in claude-code-wiki/raw/transcripts/, then reset state file so next Sunday picks up real titles.
close_reason: Rewrote check-claude-code-videos.py to use YouTube RSS feeds (xml.etree.ElementTree, no new deps) — iterates entries newest-first, stops at last seen video_id, ingests oldest-first so state ends on newest. URL-dedup in add_to_database now returns True/False so we skip wiki export when DB insert is a duplicate. Discovered the original channel_ids in the config were wrong/stale (all returned 404 from RSS) — resolved real channel_ids from each @handle and updated. Cleaned up 9 bogus DB rows (270-273 from 2026-05-10, 289-293 from 2026-05-15), 500 segment rows, 10 wiki .md files. Reset state file. Re-ran with new code: 23 real videos ingested across all 5 channels with correct titles ('Make the PERFECT Videos with Claude Code', 'Claude Code Has Evolved', etc.).

title: Fix daily AI news cron failing since May 3 (Dropbox+venv deadlock)
description: The 9 AM com.jarvis.news-aggregator launchd job has been failing daily since 2026-05-03. Last successful digest: 2026-05-02. Root cause: Python venv lives inside Dropbox CloudStorage; macOS launchd hits 'OSError: [Errno 11] Resource deadlock avoided' loading urllib3 from the venv, and 'Operation not permitted' sourcing email-config.sh from the same Dropbox path. Fix: recreate venv at ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/venv (local-only), move email-config.sh to local path, update /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh to use the new paths.
close_reason: Recreated venv at ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/venv, moved email-config.sh to ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/email-config.sh (mode 0600), updated /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh to point at new paths. Tested end-to-end: digest for 2026-05-15 generated (42 articles) and email sent successfully. launchd job reloaded.

Channels: a16z, Lex Fridman, Dwarkesh Patel, plus add Ezra Klein/Hard Fork. tryTranscript() already shells out to it; just needs the lib.

title: ai-futures: Reddit fetcher needs OAuth app
description: Reddit now returns 403 to unauthenticated www.reddit.com/*.json from this network. The reddit adapter in agent-sdk/src/fetchers/ai-futures.ts is built but blocked. Register a Reddit script-type OAuth app, add client_id/secret to .env, and switch the adapter to the OAuth token endpoint (or PRAW via a python helper). Subs: r/Futurology, r/artificial, r/singularity, r/MachineLearning. Free tier sufficient.

close_reason: M15 R4 locked — pullback bridge to Skills lands clean. Module complete.

title: Module 14 — Where MCP Breaks — the failure modes
description: Part 2 segment 5 of 6. The dark counterpart to Module 13. Inherits the positive closer and pivots to honest limitations. Four failure modes where MCP is not the right fit: wrong scale (overkill for simple cases), server quality risk (community MCP servers vary in quality), latency overhead (extra protocol hops), debug opacity (failures hard to diagnose). Visual: same showcase carousel structure as M13 but with darker/amber visual language signaling tension. Closer: 'MCP isn't always the answer.'
close_reason: Module 14 R1 locked — all 7 keyframes verified clean. Amber-only register works as dark counterpart to M13.

title: Module 13 — Where MCP Shines — when MCP is the right choice
description: Part 2 segment 4 of 6. Inherits Module 12's closer (cost is real but manageable). Justifies the MCP investment by showing concrete use cases where MCP wins. Strong fits: universal connectivity, third-party ecosystem leverage, multi-agent platforms sharing tools, production stability via standard protocol. Visual treatment: showcase carousel of use cases, each its own moment. Different aesthetic from Module 12's cost dashboard.
close_reason: Module 13 R4 — locked. Showcase carousel architecture (88s, 9MB). Three new vocabulary patterns built and folded into skill: showcase-carousel, marketplace-grid, version-contract-stamp. Four use cases (Universal connectivity / Ecosystem leverage / Multi-agent platforms / Production stability) landed cleanly with distinct visualizations. Closer fixed by hiding 2x2 grid entirely (anti-pattern A2).

title: Module 12 — What MCP Costs — the cost surface
description: Part 2 segment 3 of 6. Inherits Module 11's three primitives. Teaches the cost surface specific to MCP: every tool schema occupies tokens in context, every list-tools response is in context, costs accumulate as you add servers. Frames the trade-off: MCP gives universal connectivity, but pays in tokens. Will likely first-build the numeric-reveal-flip pattern from to-build vocabulary (the dramatic before/after number transition).
close_reason: Module 12 R4 — locked. Single-composition cost-dashboard architecture. 9 scenes, 105s. Four new vocabulary patterns built and folded into skill: context-window-horizon-bar, running-token-counter, cost-badge, numeric-reveal-flip. Mitigation beat (30K → 6K with lazy-loading) landed clean per Terry's request. Text-on-text overlap (bundles vs counter) fixed by moving bundles to top:680.

title: Module 11 — How MCP Works — protocol mechanics
description: Part 2 segment 2 of 6. Inherits Module 10 end state. Teaches the actual MCP protocol mechanics: JSON-RPC messaging, the lifecycle (initialize → list capabilities → invoke), and the three primitives (Tools / Resources / Prompts). Visual treatment: VS Code IDE simulation + terminal session + animated JSON-RPC message flow — different aesthetic from Module 10's editorial type-on-cream. First-builds for the vscode-ide-simulation and terminal-session vocabulary patterns (currently to-build) — they flip to proven on completion.
close_reason: Module 11 R2 — locked. Scene-based composition (105s, 7.5MB). Four new vocabulary patterns built and folded into skill: vscode-ide-simulation, terminal-session, page-wipe-transition, primitive-card-trio. VS Code chrome + JSON-RPC terminal exchange + Three Primitives (Tools/Resources/Prompts) cards land cleanly. Minor: terminal empty-line spacing tighter than needed, sketchy callout arrow approximate. Acceptable for shipping.

title: Module 10 redo — scene-based clips with full-frame product facsimiles
description: Critical pivot. Module 10 R2 fell into the static-composition trap — persistent header/row/center with elements animating IN PLACE. Terry's directive: push pages and large graphics in and out. Use HyperFrames native clip pattern (data-start/data-duration on outer scene wrappers) for full-frame scene transitions. Each beat is its own dominant visual scene, not a layer in a single composition. Pull pulse-ring logos (helper recipe 7), liquid glass cards (recipe 1), curved underlines (recipe 4), multi-clip cut-editing (recipe 6) from the newly-installed hyperframes-helper skill.
close_reason: Module 10 R5 — final. Scene-based 7-clip composition: Inherit / Title Splash / Bespoke Grid (12 wires) / MCP In The Middle (7 wires through hub — the Anthropic+contrast scene Terry requested) / Ecosystem / Glass IDE Call / Synthesis extended through end. Brand glyph alpha cleanup landed. Segment indicator card removed per Terry. Render: 12.2 MB, 110s. Open issue: Scene 5 cascade timing still overlaps — minor follow-up.

title: Module 10 — MCP segment 1 — What MCP Is
description: First Part 2 module. Opens by inheriting Module 09's chapter-card end-state and expanding into a deep-dive shape. Teaches: what MCP is, the protocol shape, agent ↔ server ↔ tool wire-up. Will likely first-build at least one of: cursor-click-inline-reveal, terminal-session, vscode-ide-simulation, or annotated-screenshot (depending on visual story). Each first-build flips the corresponding vocabulary entry from to-build to proven. Critical: lean on type-as-design and flow diagrams with floating labels — minimize cards/containers per the 'earn every container' directive.
notes: Module 10 R2 will be superseded by the scene-based redo. Keep R2 file for reference but new render lands in same location.
close_reason: Module 10 — MCP segment 1 — locked. 110s render at 10MB. Five new vocabulary patterns shipped and folded back into byrddynasty-video-production skill: chapter-card-dissolve-to-title, flow-node-with-floating-label, animated-flow-arc, sketchy-annotation-arrow, monospace-on-stage. R2 critique pass clean (no text-on-text, no box-on-box, no orphan elements). Anthropic + adoption framing front-and-center in Beat 4. Type-led shape established as Part 2 visual contract.

title: Part 2 — 24 modules across MCP / Skills / CLI / Code Exec
description: Build out the Part 2 deep dives: 6 modules per mechanism (×4 mechanisms = 24 modules). Six-segment skeleton per mechanism: (1) What it is, (2) How it works, (3) What it costs, (4) Where it shines, (5) Where it breaks, (6) Practical verdict. MCP modules built sequentially first to establish deep-dive shape and prove vocabulary patterns; Skills + CLI + Code Exec parallelize after MCP locks. Craft directive: fewer boxes — earn every container. Lead with type, motion, lines, negative space.

title: Fix weekly arxiv-research cron — launchd can't exec Dropbox-hosted wrapper
description: com.jarvis.arxiv-research launchd job fails every run with: /bin/bash: /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/arxiv-research-aggregator/weekly-arxiv.sh: Operation not permitted. Same root cause as jarvis-d18 — macOS launchd can't execute files inside Dropbox CloudStorage. Last failure 2026-05-10. Fix: relocate weekly-arxiv.sh to /Users/terrybyrd/bin/ (or another local path), update the plist's ProgramArguments to point at the new location. Verify any venv it uses is also local.
close_reason: Created /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-arxiv-weekly.sh (local), set PYTHON=/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 with absolute SCRIPT_DIR pointing to Dropbox source. Updated plist ProgramArguments to invoke the local wrapper directly. Reloaded launchd. Manual test ran cleanly (only a transient HTTP 429 from arXiv API rate-limiting, unrelated to the fix).

title: Fix weekly academic-research cron — launchd can't exec Dropbox-hosted wrapper
description: com.jarvis.academic-research launchd job fails every run with: /bin/bash: /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/academic-research-aggregator/weekly-research.sh: Operation not permitted. Same root cause as jarvis-d18 — macOS launchd can't execute files inside Dropbox CloudStorage. Last failure 2026-05-10. Fix: relocate weekly-research.sh to /Users/terrybyrd/bin/ (or another local path), update the plist's ProgramArguments to point at the new location. Verify any venv it uses is also local.
notes: Wrapper script /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/academic-research-aggregator/weekly-research.sh contains 'read -p "Press Enter when you're ready to continue..."' and launches Chrome interactively expecting Auburn Library login. This was never going to run from launchd — it's fundamentally an interactive script. Also cd's into /Users/terrybyrd/Dropbox/jarvis/repos/VIDEO-GENERATOR_FOUNDATIONS (a separate legacy non-CloudStorage Dropbox path). DECISION NEEDED: (a) unload launchd job and treat as manual-only, (b) split into non-interactive variant that assumes Chrome+session is already running, or (c) rewrite to use a non-Auburn data source. Until then, the cron is a no-op.
close_reason: Per user 2026-05-15: dispensing with Auburn EBSCO source — new articles are rare enough that manual checking is faster than maintaining the interactive Chrome+Auburn-login pipeline. May revisit with an API-based source (Semantic Scholar, etc.) later. Unloaded launchd job and renamed plist to com.jarvis.academic-research.plist.disabled so it won't auto-load on next login. Source scripts in skills/academic-research-aggregator/ left in place for future reference.

description: Books give framing, not ingestible full text. Build a one-off generator that queries Google Books API + Open Library for recent AI/strategy/society titles and writes a curated wiki/reading-map.md (title, author, thesis, lens). Not a streaming feed. Free, no key required.

value: DNA RULE: Every Byrddynasty video's final CTA scene MUST explicitly ask viewers to (1) subscribe to the channel, (2) like the video, (3) ring the notification bell. Generic 'subscribe for more' is NOT enough — make it compelling and a true call to action. Apply to every script + every HEYGEN-PASTE.md without being reminded. MCP video Scene 24 is the proven template.

For all future Jarvis/Byrddynasty videos, cinematic WebGL / layered depth is the default visual language. Screenshots, web-rolls, Codex Sites, code, diagrams, data cards, and B-roll should be arranged as layered spatial compositions with camera pushes, parallax, glow, masked reveals, and scroll-chapter motion. Preserve variety and real proof; do not reduce videos to generic floating cards.

Daily AI News Digest issue on 2026-06-08: reports for Jun 6-8 existed but emails were not received. Direct SMTP send of reports/news-digests/ai-news-digest-2026-06-08.md succeeded. Hardened skills/news-aggregator/launchd/jarvis-news-daily.sh and installed /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh: explicit log redirection, local email config required, email failures exit nonzero, and Phase 2B reindex runs in background so it cannot block digest email.

Research from hyperframes.dev public templates/API: official examples include Notion/Dribbble/Stripe/Raycast product showcases, fitness app, Spotify bento, X post overlay, UI 3D reveal, product promo, and community projects. Useful patterns for Jarvis videos: split long videos into short sub-compositions with explicit beat timings; use continuous canvas scenes when one visual evolves across several captions; combine background texture/sweep layers, hero objects, floating secondary objects, code/terminal/UI panels, and captions as separate tracks; prefer 3D/perspective reveal, bento pan, code-card reveal, cursor/typing/canvas interaction, and layered product-proof-trust beats over static text cards. For Video 12, this reinforces building each 10-18s subsegment as a separate visual shot with one dominant action plus optional captions, rather than one giant overlay file.

Canonical .agents/skills/jarvis-video-production now includes episode command-center templates under templates/episode-command-center/ and a scaffold script at scripts/scaffold-command-center.sh. Use it at video kickoff to create EPISODE.md, RESEARCH-BRIEF.md, CLAIM-SOURCE-MAP.md, VISUAL-TREATMENT-BOARD.md, ASSET-INVENTORY.md, SCENE-STATUS.md, QC-REPORT.md, YOUTUBE-PACKAGE.md, and scenes/.

Codex Sites are now documented in .agents/skills/jarvis-video-production as supporting production surfaces, not replacements for HyperFrames. Use Sites for dashboards, simulators, evidence explorers, command centers, review boards, comparison tools, interactive data stories, and companion resources; capture Site interactions/screenshots into HyperFrames for final VO-synced video composition.

All Jarvis/Byrddynasty YouTube video scripts should end with a dedicated CTA beat that asks viewers to subscribe, like the video, and ring the notification bell, plus a brief encouraging line about why subscribing helps them keep building with practical AI systems. This should be included by default in final VO/HeyGen paste scripts unless Terry explicitly requests no CTA.

QC RULE — 5-SECOND MAX STATIC DISPLAY: No static visual state on screen for more than 5 seconds in any Byrddynasty video. Continuous motion required — text reveals, new elements entering, micro-animations, color pulses, position shifts. Applies to every scene, including hero cards and pivots. B-roll cutaways used only as transitions ≤5s, never extended. Also: ALWAYS run real frame-extraction QC (ffmpeg seek + screenshot every 3s) before declaring a master done. Inspect that no scene goes blank during VO and no scene is more text than visual. Verify thumbnail badges/labels don't bleed through (e.g. SKILLS EPISODE, CLI EPISODE). Lock for all future agents.

HeyGen MCP connector (URL: https://mcp.heygen.com/mcp/v1/, OAuth, added to Claude.ai/Desktop) exposes create_speech which generates TTS audio in Terry's exact avatar voice (voiceId f7b9a25a75734547a3c749f7a0da3f21, the default_voice_id attached to all his private avatars including Terry Byrd digital twin 191c21c2e40d41ceb226836c37b71864). VERIFIED 2026-05-25: create_speech costs ZERO premium credits on Creator plan — tested 6 renders totaling ~120s of audio, premium credits unchanged. By contrast, create_video_from_avatar costs ~1 premium credit per minute (2.35s test cost 2 credits, min billing unit). WORKFLOW for Byrddynasty videos: (1) Use create_video_from_avatar ONLY for segments where the talking head is actually visible on screen, (2) Use create_speech for all VO-only segments — same voice, free, no British-accent ElevenLabs/Cartesia clone problem, (3) Mix both in editor over B-roll/HyperFrames/slates. This solves Terry's biggest production pain point: he was burning Avatar IV/V credits on segments where the avatar was hidden behind overlays. DELIVERY STYLE LIMITATION: create_speech runs on Starfish engine which has NO user-tunable settings — Studio's 'Direct Voice - Excited' preset is NOT exposed via API. Only speed (0.5-2.0) is adjustable. SSML inputType is accepted but Starfish may silently ignore prosody/emphasis tags — Terry's ear is the test. Terry prefers 'Direct - Excited' style for energy. RECOMMENDED HYBRID PATTERN: use MCP create_speech for neutral/explanatory segments (~80% of video, free, slightly flatter delivery), use Studio with 'Direct - Excited' preset for high-energy hook/CTA segments. Tools to load: mcp__e3751c24-e167-4e47-9168-36f7b599d6e5__create_speech, mcp__e3751c24-e167-4e47-9168-36f7b599d6e5__create_video_from_avatar, mcp__e3751c24-e167-4e47-9168-36f7b599d6e5__list_avatar_looks, mcp__e3751c24-e167-4e47-9168-36f7b599d6e5__get_video, mcp__e3751c24-e167-4e47-9168-36f7b599d6e5__get_current_user. create_speech returns audio_url (WAV), duration, and word-level timestamps for caption sync.

Canonical video production workflow is now .agents/skills/jarvis-video-production/ for Codex and Claude Code. It supersedes legacy repo skills/video-production for default video work and skills/video-image-creation for full videos. Faceless HyperFrames/mixed-register videos are default; talking-head/avatar and still-image-only workflows are legacy/special cases.

The canonical .agents/skills/jarvis-video-production skill now includes mirrored detailed references: references/v14/ for Video 14 palette, tooling, shot vocabulary, Runway prompts, anti-patterns, and length rules; references/channel-library/ for the older Claude-local Byrddynasty channel identity, shape catalog, recipes, and interaction vocabulary. Claude-local ~/.claude/skills/byrddynasty-video-v14/SKILL.md was converted to a compatibility pointer back to the repo skill.

For Video 12 and future Jarvis YouTube videos, use HyperFrames as the default visual system excluding talking heads. Prefer simulated IDEs, terminals, dashboards, code, diagrams, meters, and technical workflows in HyperFrames so objects can be timed, highlighted, transformed, and transitioned. Use real screen recordings only when believability/source evidence requires actual software behavior. Use still images only for complex one-off art or real screenshots, and animate them with tile/slice/parallax/push/annotation motion rather than presenting static slides. Each narrative segment should usually have 2-3 visual beats with builds, reveals, transformations, comparisons, evidence, or escalation; avoid reverting to one static dark box/text scene.

For Video 12, use a detailed Visual Treatment Board before production. Each subsegment should list concrete screen-action options, not just treatment categories: what appears, what moves, what changes over time, what the viewer should understand, likely production tool, and a recommendation. The board should consider Veo, source capture, simulated UI/IDE, still+motion, talking head overlays, and HyperFrames/Remotion rather than assuming any single tool.

HyperFrames/video production rule: before showing a proof or final cut, run self-QA for text collision and visual legibility. Check contact sheets and/or frame grabs for text too close to other text, text overlapping boxes, text hidden behind panels, and captions competing with foreground graphics; fix these issues before user review.

For Jarvis/Dr. Terry Byrd YouTube video dialogue, use collective voice by default: we/our/ours instead of I/my/mine. The only planned first-person singular exception is the opening avatar self-introduction, e.g. 'I am the avatar for Dr. Terry Byrd...' CTAs should appear as their own pre-closing segment before the final full-screen talking-head close.

June 9 AI News Digest failure: launchd could not write wrapper stdout to Dropbox/CloudStorage logs, causing /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh to fail at exec redirection before aggregation. Fixed by moving LaunchAgent StandardOutPath/StandardErrorPath and wrapper LOG_DIR to /Users/terrybyrd/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/logs in both repo plist/wrapper and installed ~/Library/LaunchAgents + ~/bin copies. Reloaded LaunchAgent and kickstarted it; run exited 0, generated reports/news-digests/ai-news-digest-2026-06-09.md, stored 53 articles, and sent email successfully.

For Video 12 and future Jarvis videos, create the visual story before finalizing VO. The preferred order is concept -> visual sequence -> VO that explains the visual -> exact timing -> HyperFrames build. Most 25-35 second segments should use 2 subsegments of 10-18 seconds; use 3 only when the concept naturally has three steps. Visuals may lead VO by 0.5-1.5 seconds. Each subsegment must have a system action and must make basic sense muted; avoid boxes-with-text scenes.
